subject: [PATCH 5/5] mm: memcg: separate slab stat accounting from objcg charge cache

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch's optimization for IRQ allocations/frees is unlikely to provide a significant benefit due to their rarity and suggested avoiding code complication unless proven otherwise.
reviews.raw_body:
Yes this makes sense. We already have at least one evidence (bug Hao fixed) that
these are very rare, so optimizing for such cases will just increase complexity
without real benefit.

2026-03-03.patch_summary: This patch separates slab stat accounting from object charge cache in memory cgroup (memcg) to prevent potential issues when refilling the byte stock, which could lead to excess refill of page stock and subsequent draining back to page counters.

2026-03-04.patch_summary: This patch separates slab stat accounting from object charge cache in memory cgroup (memcg) to prevent potential issues when refilling the byte stock, which could lead to excess refill of page stock and subsequent draining back to page counters.
